rationale: Neither candidate catches the ground truth's actual concern (trust_remote_code kwarg breaking compatibility with locked kernels<0.15.0 installs) — both only note that the PR description mentions trust_remote_code while the visible diff doesn't contain it, which is a valid but tangential observation, not the substantive dependency-compatibility bug. Both reviews are grounded with no fabrications. Y edges out X slightly: its test-coverage finding (test plan cites a test that doesn't exercise the new cache path) is a concrete, useful, well-cited addition, and its line citations are more precise, though its docstring nitpick is low-value; X's lock-contention observation is a good but explicitly 'optional' finding.
